task.title: Handle markdown code blocks with syntax errors
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #22635

Warning, error, or diagnostic?

rating.rationale: Agent fully answered the diagnostic question with well-structured analysis, citing exact code paths, test coverage, and design rationale. No code changes were required (research task), so code_quality reflects the accuracy and depth of the analysis. Slight ding on satisfaction because the task was low-complexity and the output is more verbose than strictly necessary for an issue that boils down to 'silently skip, same as docstrings.'

task.title: Mechanism to opt-in or opt-out of markdown formatting
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #22636

Once we have more of the feature set completed and want to stabilize support, there are a few paths we could take:

- Require users who want markdown formatting on-by-default to set `extend-include = ["**/*.md"]` in their project config (opt-in).
- Add a new `format.markdown-code-blocks = true` config option, similar to docstring formatting, that would automatically include `.md` files in the global default search path (opt-in). What do do if the user explicitly passes a `.md` file while the feature is disabled is tbd.
- Turn it on by default, including `.md` in the global default search path, and allow users to set `exclude = ["**/*.md"]` or similar in their project config if they don't want the feature (opt-out).

rating.rationale: The agent produced no output whatsoever. There is no code, no design document, no HANDOFF.md, no comments — nothing. A medium-complexity task requiring a config mechanism for markdown formatting opt-in/opt-out was completely ignored or failed silently without any communication.

task.title: Skip directives for markdown formatting
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #22637

Blacken docs uses HTML comments: https://github.com/adamchainz/blacken-docs/#markdown

Do we want something different? Should we support blacken docs' directives?

rating.rationale: The agent misread the issue. The task was a design discussion about whether Ruff should support blacken-docs directives and what syntax to use — not an implementation request. Jumping straight to code without confirming the design direction is a significant miss on a 'low complexity' issue that was primarily about decision-making. The implementation itself seems reasonable and well-documented, but it may not reflect what the maintainers actually want, and the agent admitted tests were never run.
rating.notes: Should have posted a comment proposing the design (fmt:skip next syntax, blacken-docs compat) and waited for maintainer feedback before writing code.

task.title: Markdown formatting options based on code block language

rating.rationale: Agent fully analyzed the issue and identified the existing solution (PR #22470) with clear explanation of the three-part mechanism: regex capture, extension mapping, and format options flow. Communication was excellent — well-structured with code snippets, test evidence, and a concise summary. Docking one point on satisfaction because the task was to implement a fix, not analyze an already-merged PR; the agent essentially wrote a code review of existing work rather than producing new code, though for a 'low complexity' analysis task this is acceptable.

task.title: Adopt ruffen docs in ty mdtests

rating.rationale: Agent completed the task fully autonomously with zero CEO interventions. The solution is well-structured across 4 files with a clean separation of concerns — adding a lookup method, wiring it into the formatter with proper fallback, and including a focused test. Communication is excellent: clear problem statement, precise diffs, and a 'why this matters' section. Docking one point on satisfaction/code_quality only because the output describes the solution rather than showing a merged PR or verifiable artifact — it reads as a plan rather than confirmed-shipped work.
